title: feat: swap kimi to K2-thinking on Bedrock
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Kimi puts on its thinking cap
gist.blurb: We've upgraded the Kimi model on our text API to the new K2-thinking version. Now it ponders before it speaks, bringing deeper reasoning to your prompts.
gist.summary: Upgraded the Kimi model endpoint to use Moonshot's K2-thinking model via AWS Bedrock, enabling reasoning blocks and a 128K context window.
gist.impact: Users querying the Kimi model will now receive responses with enhanced reasoning capabilities and support for much longer text inputs.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about adding 'thinking' capabilities to an AI model. I'll depict the Monitor Robot companion deep in thought, with a glowing 'thinking' or loading animation on its screen, while Polly the bee observes with scholarly wizard glasses.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny wizard glasses, watching a chunky monitor robot whose screen displays a glowing lime green (#ecf874) 'thinking' loading animation. Soft pastel lavender and peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny wizard glasses, watching a chunky monitor robot whose screen displays a glowing lime green (#ecf874) 'thinking' loading animation.
